title: Use Execute AGV Command For System Recovery
summary: Use the Execute AGV Command control in the AGVs section of the System HMI API screen for system recovery when the system has gone down and messages were missed. The source documents the control and its purpose, but does not provide the command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during system recovery when the system went down and messages were missed, and the recovery action involves the Execute AGV Command control in the AGVs section of the API screen.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete command-entry procedure when the required command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior are needed but not available from approved site documentation or support personnel.
Do not use this runbook for non-recovery AGV operations not supported by the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the System HMI API screen, locate the AGVs section, identify the Execute AGV Command control, and use it only for the documented recovery purpose when messages were missed after a system down event.

safety_notes: This is a recovery-related control with potential system-wide impact.
The source does not provide command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior; do not improvise unsupported recovery commands.
Escalate if approved site documentation or support personnel cannot provide the required recovery command details.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the System HMI API screen
AGVs section of the API screen

access_required: Access to the System HMI API screen
system_preconditions: The system went down and messages were missed.

visual_references.description: AGV API Controls screen showing the AGVs section and the Execute AGV Command control.
ACB API screen used to access system, AGV, and tote controls.

steps.title: Open the AGVs section of the API screen
steps.purpose: Reach the AGV API Controls area where the Execute AGV Command control is located.
steps.instruction: Open the System HMI API screen and navigate to the AGVs section.
steps.expected_result: The AGVs section of the API screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV API Controls area is displayed and the AGV controls are available for review.
steps.failure_condition: The API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed or is not visible.

actions.label: Open API screen
actions.exact_action: Open the System HMI API screen and navigate to the AGVs section.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The ACB API screen interface used to access AGV controls.
The AGV API Controls area within the AGVs section of the API screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The System HMI API screen cannot be accessed.
The AGVs section is not available on the API screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users press API on the "ACB System" screen to access this screen. From this ACB API interface, the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes can be controlled.
Figure 4-12, titled AGV API Controls, is associated with the AGVs section of the API screen.

steps.title: Locate the Execute AGV Command control
steps.purpose: Identify the specific recovery-related control documented by the source.
steps.instruction: In the AGVs section, locate the Execute AGV Command control.
steps.expected_result: The Execute AGV Command control is identified on the AGV API Controls screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Execute AGV Command control is visible in the AGV API Controls area.
steps.failure_condition: The Execute AGV Command control cannot be found in the AGVs section.

actions.label: Find Execute AGV Command
actions.exact_action: Locate the Execute AGV Command control in the AGVs section.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Execute AGV Command control listed among the AGV API Controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Execute AGV Command control is not present on the AGV API Controls screen.
The displayed screen does not match the documented AGV API Controls layout.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV API Controls screen documents controls for Remove AGV, Recover AGV, Pause All AGVs, Resume All AGVs, Execute AGV Command, and Resend Geek Command.

steps.title: Use Execute AGV Command for documented recovery purpose
steps.purpose: Apply the control only for the recovery use case explicitly described by the source.
steps.instruction: Use Execute AGV Command as part of system recovery when the system went down and messages were missed. If the required command details are not available from approved site documentation or support personnel, stop and escalate.
steps.expected_result: The Execute AGV Command control is used for the documented system recovery purpose related to missed messages.
steps.healthy_condition: The control is used only in the documented recovery scenario and the operator has approved recovery command details from site documentation or support personnel.
steps.failure_condition: Required command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior are unknown, unavailable, or unsupported by the source.

actions.label: Use Execute AGV Command for recovery
actions.exact_action: Use Execute AGV Command as part of system recovery when messages were missed after the system went down.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Execute AGV Command control used for system recovery in the AGV API Controls screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source does not provide the command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior for this control.
Required recovery command details are not available from approved site documentation or support personnel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Execute AGV Command: This is used for a system recover in the event that the system goes down and messages are missed.

success_criteria: The AGVs section of the API screen is accessed.
The Execute AGV Command control is identified.
The control is used only for the documented recovery case where the system went down and messages were missed.
healthy_conditions: The System HMI API screen and AGVs section are accessible.
The Execute AGV Command control is visible on the AGV API Controls screen.
Approved recovery command details are available when needed from site documentation or support personnel.
failure_conditions: The API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed.
The Execute AGV Command control cannot be located.
The source does not provide enough detail to safely determine command inputs, parameters, or confirmation behavior.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required recovery command details are not available from approved site documentation or support personnel.
Escalate if the Execute AGV Command control is unavailable or the screen does not match the documented AGV API Controls view.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV API Controls screen showing the Execute AGV Command control.
ACB API screen used to access AGV controls.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Execute AGV Command: This is used for a system recover in the event that the system goes down and messages are missed.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact command inputs for Execute AGV Command.
The source does not provide required parameters or field values for the control.
The source does not describe confirmation behavior, expected on-screen feedback, or completion indicators.
The source does not define whether production must be stopped before using this control.
The source does not define whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this recovery action.
